summary: Assign the corresponding materials to the mold components. Each material contains its own thermophysical properties, which will be used by PoligonSoft during the calculation.
actions:
On the Modes tab, switch to Volume Indices. In this mode, each color corresponds to a volume index, that is, to the material assigned to the component. Download the material template and save it on your computer. Materials can also be assigned one by one from the database, but for real work it is more practical to use a template with common materials. In the Model Tree, switch to the Project Overview tab. Find the Mold Materials line and click the three-dot button to open the template import dialog. Select the downloaded file and click Open. Review the list of imported materials. Each material will have a number, corresponding to the volume index, and an assigned color. Return to the Model Tree. Double-click the first Mold component. In the pop-up window, assign the corresponding material and click OK. Repeat the operation for all Mold components. The selected element will be shown as active in the workspace, while the other elements will appear semi-transparent.
In this lesson, the large sleeves correspond to isothermic sleeves, the small sleeves correspond to exothermic sleeves, and the chills correspond to GS45 steel. Also assign a clear name to each component to make the following steps easier. For convenience, merge all elements with the same material into a single volume. Hold down the CTRL key and select all chills in the Model Tree.
Right-click and run Merge Volumes. The selected elements will be merged into a single volume in the Model Tree. Repeat the same operation for the isothermic sleeves and for the exothermic sleeves.
expected: The mold components will have the correct materials assigned. Elements with the same material will be grouped into single volumes, making the next project setup steps easier.
